title: Link-Bearing Elements, Base URLs, Relations, Discovery, and Scheduling · Code Recall
summary: A delayed crawl review reconstructs link-bearing elements, base urls, relations, discovery, and scheduling from authorization, revision, seed, URL key, response identity, robots decision, parse record, extraction provenance, frontier event, report row, failure, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for typed link-discovery record after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Extract navigational, embedded, form, metadata, refresh, and structured-data URLs by element and attribute with distinct policies.
Apply effective response URL and the first valid HTML base element deliberately and retain both source and resolved references.
Interpret rel tokens such as canonical, alternate, next, prev, nofollow, sponsored, and ugc as evidence or hints, not universal commands.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

stimulus.title: Link Provenance case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review site-quality team handles case 2539 during a privacy-minimized internal crawl. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate link types, base resolution, relation semantics, admission order, deduplication, and discovery provenance; no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted; then replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget. lines.label: risk

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Retain parent page, node or metadata source, raw reference, resolved URL, relation, anchor context, decision, and rejection reason. Artifact under review: typed link-discovery record.

options.text: The Link Provenance record says typed link-discovery record looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Link Provenance.

For separate evidence from confidence, crawl one convenient page, omit owner authorization, revision, URL and robots policy, response budgets, parser and selector limits, provenance, frontier and concurrency evidence, changed fixtures, privacy, security, accessibility, stop, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as crawler proof. This does not satisfy Link Provenance for typed link-discovery record.
The record names the assumption, applies Link Provenance, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
